requiredExam.label: Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation

source.source_section: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue. Hyperpigmentation supports primary adrenal insufficiency or ACTH excess and changes ACTH, antibody, renin/aldosterone, or steroid-replacement interpretation.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa, palmar creases, scars, and sun-protected skin for diffuse or patchy hyperpigmentation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When primary adrenal insufficiency, Addison disease, ACTH excess, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Hyperpigmentation supports primary adrenal insufficiency or ACTH excess and changes ACTH, antibody, renin/aldosterone, or steroid-replacement interpretation.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion. Dry mucous membranes change adrenal-crisis severity assessment, fluid urgency, and stress-dose steroid planning.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting, vomiting, or poor intake is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.limitations: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.technique: Ask the patient to rise from a chair without using arms if safe, observing proximal strength and need for assistance.

requiredExam.label: Measure blood pressure

source.source_section: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) curated endocrine workup: basic bedside data and safety checks
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context
requiredExam.management_change: Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
requiredExam.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.technique: Measure blood pressure using an appropriately sized cuff after a brief rest period, seated or supine as clinically appropriate.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).

requiredExam.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Tachycardia, bradycardia, or irregular rhythm changes acuity, ECG need, and treatment urgency.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, or acute physiologic stress
requiredExam.management_change: Tachycardia, bradycardia, or irregular rhythm changes acuity, ECG need, and treatment urgency.
requiredExam.limitations: Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.technique: Palpate or monitor heart rate for a full interval, noting rate and regularity.

requiredExam.management_change: Orthostasis changes crisis concern, fluid strategy, and hormone replacement urgency.
requiredExam.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.technique: Measure supine or seated and standing blood pressure and heart rate to assess for an orthostatic drop.
requiredExam.rationale: Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).

requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.management_change: Altered mental status changes urgency, disposition, and need for monitored evaluation.

conditionalExam.label: Palpate abdomen for tenderness

source.source_section: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) curated endocrine workup: conditional bedside maneuver add-ons
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.action: Add when adrenal crisis, vomiting, abdominal pain, shock is present; this evaluates abdominal complication, endocrine crisis mimic, or precipitating illness. Tenderness changes adrenal crisis severity and mimic evaluation.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets abdominal complication, endocrine crisis mimic, or precipitating illness; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect and palpate the abdomen gently, localizing tenderness and stopping if guarding, rebound, or severe pain occurs.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When adrenal crisis, vomiting, or abdominal pain is possible
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: abdominal complication, endocrine crisis mimic, or precipitating illness
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Tenderness changes adrenal crisis severity and mimic evaluation.
conditionalExam.limitations: Palpate abdomen for tenderness is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

reference_values: 17-OHP thresholds vary by age, gestational age, assay, cycle phase, and timing; morning follicular sampling improves adult NCCAH screening.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: For suspected classic CAH or crisis, check electrolytes, glucose, cortisol/ACTH context, 17-hydroxyprogesterone, renin/aldosterone, and treat instability immediately.
For nonclassic CAH, screen with early morning follicular 17-hydroxyprogesterone and confirm with cosyntropin-stimulated testing when borderline.
Interpret 17-OHP by age, gestational age, sex, assay, timing, menstrual phase, and glucocorticoid exposure.
Use androgen profile and CYP21A2 genetics when diagnosis, family planning, or genotype-phenotype counseling is needed.
Confirm that Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.

Order the first-line tests in sequence: 17-hydroxyprogesterone, often with cosyntropin stimulation; Electrolytes, glucose, renin/aldosterone; Androgens: testosterone, androstenedione, DHEAS as indicated.

Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: 17-OHP thresholds vary by age, gestational age, assay, cycle phase, and timing; morning follicular sampling improves adult NCCAH screening.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Treat adrenal crisis or salt-wasting with stress-dose hydrocortisone, fluids, dextrose, electrolyte correction, and mineralocorticoid/salt support as indicated.
Use physiologic glucocorticoid replacement for classic CAH and fludrocortisone when mineralocorticoid deficiency is present.
For nonclassic CAH, treat symptoms such as hirsutism, acne, menstrual dysfunction, or infertility rather than asymptomatic labs alone.
Monitor growth/puberty in younger patients and adult overtreatment risks including Cushingoid effects, bone, metabolic, fertility, and adrenal-rest tumors.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH); draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
For confirmed Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.
